sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Shortness of breath
History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing shortness of breath for approximately one week. He states this symptom occurred six months ago and was managed with an increased lasix dose. He has a history of coronary artery disease with a stent placed in the right coronary artery and an ejection fraction of approximately 35%. He admits to a diet high in salt over the past few weeks due to holiday gatherings. He reports swelling in his legs and is currently taking lasix at 40 milligrams daily. He has been urinating less frequently recently and reports nocturia, sometimes waking twice a night. He reports shortness of breath at rest and when walking quickly, but is able to lay flat. He denies any recent trauma. He took motrin for foot pain a week ago and stopped as soon as it subsided. He is currently taking Tylenol.
Findings
Physical exam reveals jugular venous distension of 9 cm, bilateral lower extremity edema (1+), and a systolic ejection murmur at the left base (3/6).
Assessment
Plan
